answer: This module provides runtime support for type hints.
The Python runtime does not enforce function and variable type annotations.

A type alias declares two types to be equivalent to one another, so the static type checker treats them as exactly equivalent in all cases. In contrast, NewType declares one type to be a subtype of another, so the static type checker treats the new type as a subclass of the original, which means a value of the original type cannot be used where the new type is expected.
Special type indicating an unconstrained type.
